data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY, QQQ Pull Back as Core Inflation Cools; Tariff Revenue Shrinks
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed Tuesday's trading session in negative territory, shrugging off an encouraging inflation reading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In December, the Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose by 2.7% year-over-year, in line with the consensus estimate of 2.7% and unchanged from November. Core CPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, increased by 2.6% from a year ago, below the estimate of 2.7% and holding steady from the prior month.

Despite easing prices in recent months, inflation remains above the Fed's target of 2%. Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis President Alberto Musalem noted that the inflation data is "encouraging," although he still sees "little reason" to cut interest rates in the near term. He believes that rates are currently around a neutral level, which means that they neither stimulate nor restrict economic growth.

Investors seem to agree, with the odds of a 25 bps rate cut at the next three Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings all drifting lower in response to the inflation data. The Fed is widely expected to hold rates steady during its first FOMC meeting of the year on January 28, with CME's FedWatch tool pricing in 97.2% odds.

"JUST OUT: Great (LOW!) Inflation numbers for the USA," President Trump said in a Truth Social post on Tuesday. "That means that Jerome 'Too Late' Powell should cut interest rates, MEANINGFULLY!!!"

Trump also promised to support protesters in Iran amid an uprising over economic challenges and political repression. On Monday, he ordered a 25% tariff on all countries that trade with Iran, which could potentially fray the U.S.-China trade truce. China purchased over 80% of Iran's oil exports in 2025 and is its largest trading partner. Other key partners include Russia, India, and Japan.

Elsewhere, the U.S. budget deficit notched a monthly record high of $145 billion in December as tariff revenues fell to $27.9 billion, registering the lowest amount since July. However, it's worth noting that after adjustments, the budget would have fallen to $112 billion, marking a year-over-year decrease of 11%, or $14 billion. A major adjustment was accounting for the transfer of $32 billion in January benefit payments into December because the new year began on a weekend. Additionally, while tariff revenue has cooled in recent months, it's still up by 333% from the comparable period last year since the start of fiscal year 2026 on October 1, 2025.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.19% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 0.18%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Netflix Stock (NFLX) Jumps on Reports It Will Make All-Cash Offer for Warner Bros. Discovery
data.description:
Shares of streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) are up 1% in after-hours trading on reports the company is planning to revise its takeover bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) and make it an all-cash offer.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

WBD stock is up 2% on reports that Netflix is planning to sweeten its offer for the entertainment giant. Bloomberg was first to report that Netflix is looking to make its $83 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. an all-cash deal.

News of the revised proposal comes with Netflix embroiled in a takeover battle for Warner Bros. Discovery against Paramount Skydance (PSKY). The board of directors at Warner Bros. has endorsed Netflix's takeover offer and repeatedly rejected Paramount Skydance's bids as "inferior." However, Paramount is not going away.

The Battle for Warner Bros.

Most recently, Paramount Skydance made a hostile bid of $30 per share for all of Warner Bros. Discovery, including its legacy television networks that include CNN, HBO, and the Food Network. After the board at Warner Bros. rejected that offer, Paramount launched a lawsuit.

Paramount Skydance's lawsuit aims to force Warner Bros. to disclose all of the financial details of its $83 billion deal with Netflix. Paramount is also threatening to launch a proxy fight and take its acquisition offer directly to Warner Bros. Discovery's shareholders.

Netflix is looking to purchase Warner Bros' streaming business only, with the legacy TV networks to be spun off into a new publicly traded company. Netflix and Paramount each want to gain control of Warner Bros.' film studio and the HBO Max streaming service, as well as franchises such as "Harry Potter," "Game of Thrones," and D.C. comic book characters Superman and Batman.

Is NFLX Stock a Buy?

The stock of Netflix has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 37 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 26 Buy, nine Hold, and two Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average NFLX price target of $130.17 implies 44.97% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 'It's Time to Pounce,' Says John Vinh on AMD Stock
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) is a company adept at turning expectations on its head and has often proven the naysayers wrong. The company was, for a time, considered more or less out of the AI game, given the wide gap to segment leader Nvidia. However, that is no longer the case, with its positioning now viewed as much stronger.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That backdrop sets the stage for a reassessment from KeyBanc's John Vinh, an analyst ranked among the top 2% on Wall Street. Back in April, Vinh grew cautious, downgrading AMD from Overweight (i.e., Buy) to Sector Weight (i.e., Neutral). The move reflected concerns over a potential demand gap between the MI355 accelerator and the eventual ramp of AMD's rack-scale Helios platform powered by MI455, which is not expected to reach volume production until late 2026. However, several developments have now led Vinh to reassess that take.

For one, supply chain checks suggest that a recent surge in hyperscaler demand has left AMD close to fully sold out of server CPUs for 2026, with the company potentially considering a 10–15% price increase in 1Q26. Based on this backdrop, Vinh estimates AMD's server CPU business will grow at least 50% this year.

Meanwhile, demand for MI355 and the expected ramp of MI455 together underpin the analyst's estimate of AI revenues reaching $14 billion–$15 billion this year. Supply-chain visibility points to shipments of roughly 200,000 MI355 GPUs in 1H26, followed by a "significant ramp" of MI455 in 2H26. Upstream partners indicate a total MI455 supply of around 400,000–500,000 units, with approximately 290,000–300,000 GPUs earmarked for AMD's rack-scale Helios platform and the balance allocated to 8-GPU AI server configurations. Given the timing of the Helios ramp, it remains uncertain whether all of these GPUs can be fully deployed within 2H26. Similar to Nvidia's approach, the 5-star analyst expects AMD to recognize a meaningful portion of MI455 and Helios revenue as components sold to its ODM partner, ZT Systems, for rack builds.

The launch schedule for MI455 and Helios remains unchanged, with MI455 volume production expected to begin ramping in 3Q26 and volume Helios rack shipments anticipated in 4Q26. Vinh notes that feedback from Asia continues to indicate committed customers include OpenAI, AWS, Meta, and Microsoft. From a technical aspect, AMD has increased the MI455 power specification to 2.3kW, up from the previously expected 1.7kW, bringing it in line with Nvidia's Rubin R200, although it remains unclear whether this change was initiated by AMD or in response to Nvidia.

Lastly, while PC desktop demand is likely to underperform this year due to rising memory prices and ongoing shortages, despite the softer volume environment, the mix impact is expected to be favorable for AMD's gross margins.

Taken together, these factors have convinced Vinh to turn more constructive again. The analyst has upgraded AMD to Overweight (i.e., Buy), with a $270 price target implying 22% upside. (To watch Vinh's track record, click here)

Overall, AMD has attracted a lot of attention, picking up no fewer than 34 analyst reviews in recent months. These include 26 Buys along with 8 Holds for a Strong Buy consensus rating. The stock is selling for $220.97 and its $284.23 average price target indicates ~29% upside coming up. (See AMD stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Ford's (F) 'Eyes-Off Autonomy' Bet Leaves Stock Stuck in Neutral
data.description:
If you look at Ford (F) right now, it is hard not to feel a bit torn. On the bright side, we have a notably cleaner story taking shape, with hybrids carrying the earnings and a cheaper EV strategy. There is also a credible push into software and "eyes-off" autonomy by 2028.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On the other hand, the balance sheet remains heavy. Any stumble in execution or a change in the macro setup can hurt more than today's valuation suggests. So I end up neutral. The potential is real, the multiple looks broadly fair, but the liabilities side of the balance sheet stops this from being an easy, conviction-long story.

Eyes-Off Tech for the Many Presented at CES 2026

Last week's CES 2026 gave Ford a much-needed opportunity to turn its autonomy story into something concrete. BlueCruise, now a Level 2 hands-free system, is being developed to reach Level 3 "eyes-off" driving in 2028, launching on a new Universal Electric Vehicle (UEV) platform rather than a six-figure halo SUV. The first UEV will be a midsize electric pickup in 2027 at about $30,000, a foil to Cadillac's Escalade IQ, which starts around $130,000.
data.author: Nikolaos Sismanis

data.title: Amazon, Meta Top Tech Bets for 2026, Says Bernstein's Top Analyst
data.description:
Bernstein's top analyst Mark Shmulik named Amazon (AMZN) and Meta Platforms (META) as his top large-capitalization tech picks for 2026, after lagging the broader S&P 500 index (SPX) in 2025. For reference, AMZN stock rose 12.8% over the past year, while META gained only 5.5%, and the SPX surged about 19.4%. Both Magnificent 7 stocks are expected to outperform in 2026, with Amazon racing in the first half and Meta benefiting in the latter part of the year.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Shmulik reiterated his "Buy" rating on both stocks in a Monday client note outlining 2026 expectations. He emphasized that a central theme this year will be investors' focus pivoting from AI model performance to actual adoption of AI tools.

Shmulik is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #571 out of 10,398 analysts tracked. He has a 72% success rate and an average return per rating of 23.10%.

AMZN Stock: Project Rainier Will Drive AWS Growth in 2026

Shmulik expects Amazon to reverse the narrative that its Amazon Web Services (AWS) cloud unit trails competitors in securing AI-related business. He attributed Amazon's lackluster 2025 stock returns to sluggish AWS growth relative to peers and flat retail operating income amid tariff challenges. At the same time, he acknowledged a lack of flashy product launches, partnerships, and compute deals.

For 2026, he expects AWS to accelerate after topping net new bookings in Q3 2025, with momentum building via Project Rainier. He believes that retail margins stand to improve steadily through cost-cutting layoffs, efficiency gains, and robotics adoption. Project Rainier is AWS's massive AI supercomputer, launched in late 2025, powered by nearly 500,000 Trainium2 chips across U.S. data centers for hyperscale AI training.

Shmulik kept his $300 price target on AMZN stock, which implies 21.7% upside potential from current levels.

META Stock: Most Compelling Mag 7 Catch-Up Trade for 2026

Shmulik highlighted that Meta has the highest overall upside among the stocks he covers, although it carries "notable risk." He noted that the stock is still rebounding from post-earnings sell-off after its Q3 report on October 29, when the social media giant surprised analysts by raising its 2026 capex forecast. Shmulik stated that Meta is pouring resources into data centers, aiming to develop AI-powered superintelligence.

According to Shmulik, Meta's aggressive push into frontier-model development triggered a major reset in 2025 spending, EPS, and free-cash-flow expectations. It's a "coin flip" whether the company can catch up to peers and deliver a best-in-class model early in 2026, he added. The bear case is that Meta's model benchmarks "close-but-not-quite" to peers, forcing endless investments over an undefined timeline just to close the gap.

However, Shmulik expects Meta's intense capex growth to peak this year. The tech giant has already proven it can grow and monetize AI products without leading in large language models (LLMs). "Meta is the most compelling Magnificent Seven catch-up trade for 2026 in our eyes," Shmulik wrote, reiterating his $870 price target, implying 35.5% upside potential.

AMZN or META: Which Is the Better Tech Stock?

The TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool shows that analysts have assigned a "Strong Buy" consensus rating on both AMZN and META shares, with META offering a higher upside potential between the two.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Here's Why Wolfe Added Nvidia Stock (NVDA) to its 'Alpha List'
data.description:
Wolfe Research added semiconductor giant Nvidia (NVDA) to its "Alpha List." The firm's top analyst Chris Caso highlighted that NVDA stock has risen "only 36%" over the past year, lagging several artificial intelligence (AI)-related stocks. The top analyst explained the reasons for the underperformance and stated that he finds NVDA stock attractive at the current valuation levels.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wolfe Analyst Is Bullish on NVDA Stock

Caso listed three reasons for Nvidia stock's underperformance compared to other AI stocks like Micron Technology (MU):

  • The delayed launch of the Blackwell platform
  • Worries in the broader market about the sustainability of AI spending
  • Concerns about market share loss amid rising competition from custom AI chips

Caso contends that NVDA stock remains compelling, given that it is trading at just 23x his 2026 earnings per share (EPS) estimate, well below the 5-year average of about 35x. The analyst's bullish stance is backed by the ramp-up in Nvidia's Blackwell AI platform. Additionally, Caso highlighted the attractive specs of the Rubin platform, noting that its AI inference is 5x better than Blackwell's. Notably, Rubin is on track to ramp up in the second half of 2026.

The top-rated analyst expects an upside of at least $40 billion to the calendar year 2026 revenue consensus for Nvidia, considering the company's recent guidance. Recently, Nvidia CFO Colette Kress said that the $500 billion revenue number from Blackwell and Rubin, announced in October 2025, "has definitely gotten larger." The analyst also sees upside to estimates based on the recent comments on the pricing of Blackwell Ultra and Rubin. Furthermore, Caso sees the possibility of H200 shipments to China offering additional upside to estimates.

Interestingly, Caso sees only Alphabet-owned Google's (GOOGL) tensor processing units as a major competition for NVDA, given that other custom AI chips are not ramping as rapidly. That said, the analyst highlighted that the deployment of TPUs in Google's data centers for third-party customers could still be limited in scope.

Is NVDA Stock a Good Buy?

Overall, Nvidia scores Wall Street's Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell recommendation. The average NVDA stock price target of $264.97 indicates 43% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Percepta AI Calls Palantir's (PLTR) Lawsuit an Attempt to Crush Competition
data.description:
Executives at AI startup Percepta AI are pushing back against a lawsuit from AI software firm Palantir (PLTR) by saying that the case is really about limiting competition, not protecting secrets. In a court filing this week, Percepta's lawyers said that Palantir is trying to prevent employees from leaving and to shut down the startup before it can grow. They also argued that Palantir's post-employment agreements are too broad and should not be enforced.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In their view, Palantir is using the lawsuit to keep control over former workers and slow down rivals. Interestingly, the case began in October, when Palantir sued Percepta in federal court in New York. Palantir accused Percepta co-founders Hirsh Jain and Radha Jain, both former Palantir employees, of using stolen information to build a competing business. The company also said that the founders tried to recruit Palantir staff and take its valuable intellectual property.

In addition, Palantir accused another Percepta employee, Joanna Cohen, of emailing herself confidential files and taking pictures of sensitive data after leaving the company. Unsurprisingly, Percepta denied all of these claims in December and called the lawsuit baseless. In its latest filing, the company said that detailed searches found no Palantir materials inside Percepta's systems. It also said that the screenshots Cohen took were made in good faith to finish work tasks and would be useless to Percepta, especially now that the information is outdated.

Is PLTR Stock a Good Buy?

Overall, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on PLTR stock based on five Buys, 10 Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average PLTR price target of $192.88 per share implies 7.8% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: U.S. Budget Deficit Sets a December Record as Tariff Revenue Cools
data.description:
In December, the U.S. budget deficit rose by 67% year-over-year to $145 billion, setting a record high for the month but below the consensus estimate of $152.5 billion. In addition, December's tariff revenue was $27.9 billion, the lowest amount since July and down from $30.75 billion in November and $31.35 billion in October.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, the U.S. Treasury noted that after accounting for adjustments, the December budget deficit would have fallen by 11% from a year ago to $112 billion. One of the adjustments accounted for the transfer of $32 billion of January benefit payments into December because the new year began on a weekend.

U.S. on Track for $2 Trillion Budget Deficit in FY2026

While tariff revenue has fallen in recent months, total customs duties for fiscal year 2026, which began on October 1, 2025, have increased by 333% from the comparable period last year as a result of the Trump administration's tariffs.

Since the beginning of the fiscal year, the budget deficit has totaled $602 billion, down by 15% compared to the same period a year ago. The U.S. is on track to reach a deficit of nearly $2 trillion this fiscal year, according to Maya MacGuineas, president of the Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget. The deficit was $1.7 trillion in the 2025 calendar year, marking a three-year low.
